sections.paragraph:
pl. with suff. שִׁקְרֵיהֶם Jer 23:32, m.

(1) a lie. דִּבְרֵי שֶׁקֶר lying words, Exo 5:9. עֵד שֶׁקֶר a lying witness, Deu 19:18. נִשְׁבַּע לַשֶּׁקֶר to be perjured, Levit. 5:24 19:12 . נִכָּא בַשֶּׁקֶר to prophesy false things (notreceived from God), Jer 5:31 20:6 29:9 . Absol. and in the manner of an adverb, (thou hast spoken) falsely, (it is) a lie, 2Ki 9:12 Jer 37:14. Pl. lies, Psa 101:7. Once for concr. a liar (for אִישׁ שֶׁקֶר), Pro 17:4.

(2) whatever deceives, fraud, vanity. Psa 33:17, שֶׁקֶר הַסּוּס לִתְשׁוּעָה i.e. they are deceived who hope for victory from cavalry. Hence לַשֶּׁקֶר in vain, 1Sa 25:21 Jer 3:23 and שֶׁקֶר without cause, undeservedly, Psa 38:20 69:5 119:78 , 86 Psa 119:86.
